verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN48/TGN51) is a type I single-pass TM protein that resides constitutively in the trans-Golgi network and cycles between the TGN and early endosomes — it is an endomembrane-resident protein, not a plasma membrane protein. Its cytoplasmic tail contains TGN-retention/retrieval signals that keep it cycling within the secretory/endosomal system. No documented stable PM pool: (1) No cancer/stress ecto-display documented; (2) No narrow germline/developmental outer-leaflet display; (3) Not a lysosomal TM protein undergoing lysosomal exocytosis — it recycles to TGN before reaching lysosomes; (4) No documented PM minority pool or secretory cycling to the plasma membrane; (5) No wash-resistant TM-partner tethering at the cell surface. No surface-directed therapeutic programs known. The protein is not surface-accessible from the extracellular face.
